title: ericoila aestiva
intro: Ericoila aestiva, also known as the Desert Lavender, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its purple flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The medicinal properties of Ericoila aestiva include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of consuming Ericoila aestiva tea or supplements include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Ericoila aestiva often involve infusing dried flowers in hot water to make a tea, or using the plant in tinctures and salves for topical application.
